title: Mark Howard (producer)

Musical artist

**Mark Howard** (born June 8, 1964 in [Manchester](/wiki/Manchester "Manchester"), England) is a Canadian [record producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer"), engineer, and mixer, who has worked with artists including [Bob Dylan](/wiki/Bob_Dylan "Bob Dylan"), [Tom Waits](/wiki/Tom_Waits "Tom Waits"), [The Tragically Hip](/wiki/The_Tragically_Hip "The Tragically Hip"), [Lucinda Williams](/wiki/Lucinda_Williams "Lucinda Williams"), [Willie Nelson](/wiki/Willie_Nelson "Willie Nelson"), [Marianne Faithfull](/wiki/Marianne_Faithfull "Marianne Faithfull"), [Emmylou Harris](/wiki/Emmylou_Harris "Emmylou Harris"), [U2](/wiki/U2 "U2"), [Peter Gabriel](/wiki/Peter_Gabriel "Peter Gabriel"), [R.E.M.](/wiki/R.E.M. "R.E.M."), [Neil Young](/wiki/Neil_Young "Neil Young"), [Kaizers Orchestra](/wiki/Kaizers_Orchestra "Kaizers Orchestra"), [Lucky Dube](/wiki/Lucky_Dube "Lucky Dube"), [Fiona Apple](/wiki/Fiona_Apple "Fiona Apple") and [The Neville Brothers](/wiki/The_Neville_Brothers "The Neville Brothers").

## [[1]](#cite_note-1)Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mark_Howard_(producer)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: [1]Career")]

Howard began his career in [Hamilton, Ontario](/wiki/Hamilton,_Ontario "Hamilton, Ontario"), mixing shows in local clubs, and touring across Canada with [King Biscuit Boy](/wiki/King_Biscuit_Boy "King Biscuit Boy"). After a motorbike accident which left him unable to lift heavy equipment, he began working as an assistant at [Grant Avenue Studios](/w/index.php?title=Grant_Avenue_Studios&action=edit&redlink=1 "Grant Avenue Studios (page does not exist)").[[2]](#cite_note-octopusmediaink.com-2) In 1986, while working at Grant Avenue Studio, he met [Daniel Lanois](/wiki/Daniel_Lanois "Daniel Lanois") and began working on his solo album, *[Acadie](/wiki/Acadie_(album) "Acadie (album)")*. Lanois invited him to [New Orleans](/wiki/New_Orleans "New Orleans") to help set up a studio and begin work on [The Neville Brothers](/wiki/The_Neville_Brothers "The Neville Brothers") album *[Yellow Moon](/wiki/Yellow_Moon_(Neville_Brothers_album) "Yellow Moon (Neville Brothers album)")*, beginning a 23-year-long musical partnership. Howard worked on mixing, recording and engineering, as well as studio installations. They had studios in New Orleans, [Kingsway](/w/index.php?title=Kingsway_Studio&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kingsway Studio (page does not exist)"), Emila Court on St Charles Ave, and a Victorian mansion at 1305 Soniat St, Mexico, and in San Francisco.[[2]](#cite_note-octopusmediaink.com-2) In 1987, Howard recorded the Neville Brothers' *[Yellow Moon](/wiki/Yellow_Moon_(Neville_Brothers_album) "Yellow Moon (Neville Brothers album)")* at Emila Court. In 1989, [Bob Dylan](/wiki/Bob_Dylan "Bob Dylan")'s *[Oh Mercy](/wiki/Oh_Mercy "Oh Mercy")* was recorded on Soniat St.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/53/SoniatSt.jpeg/250px-SoniatSt.jpeg)](/wiki/File:SoniatSt.jpeg)

This is the house on Soniat St, New Orleans where [Bob Dylan](/wiki/Bob_Dylan "Bob Dylan")'s *Oh Mercy* was recorded in April, 1989 by Mark Howard.

Howard then recorded and mixed Daniel Lanois' debut solo album *[Acadie](/wiki/Acadie_(album) "Acadie (album)")*. In 1990, Howard recorded [Chris Whitley](/wiki/Chris_Whitley "Chris Whitley")'s debut album, *[Living with the Law](/wiki/Living_with_the_Law_(album) "Living with the Law (album)")* at [Kingsway Studio](/w/index.php?title=Kingsway_Studio&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kingsway Studio (page does not exist)") and in 1991 recorded and mixed Lanois' second album *[For the Beauty of Wynona](/wiki/For_the_Beauty_of_Wynona "For the Beauty of Wynona")*. In 1991, Mark began work on [R.E.M.](/wiki/R.E.M. "R.E.M.")'s *[Automatic for the People](/wiki/Automatic_for_the_People "Automatic for the People")*. Whilst at Kingsway Howard worked on records for the Neville Brothers (*Brother's Keeper*), [Peter Gabriel](/wiki/Peter_Gabriel "Peter Gabriel") (*[Us](/wiki/Us_(Peter_Gabriel_album) "Us (Peter Gabriel album)")*), R.E.M. (*[Monster](/wiki/Monster_(R.E.M._album) "Monster (R.E.M. album)")*), [Harold Budd](/wiki/Harold_Budd "Harold Budd") (*By the Dawn of the Early Light*), and [Iggy Pop](/wiki/Iggy_Pop "Iggy Pop") (*[American Caesar](/wiki/American_Caesar_(album) "American Caesar (album)")*).

In 1993, Howard went solo as a producer, producing [The Tragically Hip](/wiki/The_Tragically_Hip "The Tragically Hip")'s *[Day for Night](/wiki/Day_for_Night_(Tragically_Hip_album) "Day for Night (Tragically Hip album)")*. He was nominated at the Canadian Juno awards for producer of the year. In 1994, Howard worked on post-production for [Emmylou Harris](/wiki/Emmylou_Harris "Emmylou Harris")'s *[Wrecking Ball](/wiki/Wrecking_Ball_(Emmylou_Harris_album) "Wrecking Ball (Emmylou Harris album)")* at the studio he shared with Lanois in San Francisco. That same year, Howard recorded tracks for Lucky Dube at Peter Gabriel's [Real World Studios](/wiki/Real_World_Studios "Real World Studios"). Howard is credited as a producer on Dube's work, specifically on the 1994 compilation album *A Week Or Two In The Real World*.

Howard then opened a studio called the Teatro, in [Oxnard, California](/wiki/Oxnard,_California "Oxnard, California"),[[4]](#cite_note-4) in an abandoned 1920s theatre. At this studio Howard and Lanois co-produced the soundtrack to the movie *[Slingblade](/wiki/Slingblade "Slingblade")*,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] and Howard then produced [Chris Whitley](/wiki/Chris_Whitley "Chris Whitley")'s *Terra Incognita*. In 1995, Howard co-produced with Ian Moore the latter's second studio album, *Modernday Folklore*.

In 1996, [Bob Dylan](/wiki/Bob_Dylan "Bob Dylan") began work with Howard and Lanois on *[Time Out of Mind](/wiki/Time_Out_of_Mind_(Bob_Dylan_album) "Time Out of Mind (Bob Dylan album)")* at the Teatro,[[5]](#cite_note-5) and recorded at Miami's [Criteria Studios](/wiki/Criteria_Studios "Criteria Studios") for a month before returning to the Teatro to finish recording the album. The record went on to win three Grammys, including [Album of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Album_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Album of the Year"). In Dylan's acceptance speech, he thanked Mark Howard, commenting that "we got a particular sound on this record which you don't get every day".[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2e/TeatroStudio.jpeg/250px-TeatroStudio.jpeg)](/wiki/File:TeatroStudio.jpeg)

The Teatro studio in Oxnard, California, an abandoned theatre that Howard turned into a recording studio.

In 1997, Howard and Lanois recorded [Willie Nelson](/wiki/Willie_Nelson "Willie Nelson")'s *[Teatro](/wiki/Teatro_(Willie_Nelson_album) "Teatro (Willie Nelson album)")*, named after the studio. In July 1998, Howard produced [Marianne Faithfull](/wiki/Marianne_Faithfull "Marianne Faithfull")'s, *[Vagabond Ways](/wiki/Vagabond_Ways "Vagabond Ways")*. Faithfull was introduced to Howard by Bob Dylan, who said to her that "You know Marianne, people like us with funny voices, you have to be very careful who you let produce you."[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[U2](/wiki/U2 "U2") was the last band to record with Howard and Lanois at the Teatro, recording some of *[All That You Can't Leave Behind](/wiki/All_That_You_Can%27t_Leave_Behind "All That You Can't Leave Behind")*.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

After the Teatro Howard created his own studio at the [Paramour Estate](/wiki/Paramour_Estate "Paramour Estate") in [Silver Lake, Los Angeles](/wiki/Silver_Lake,_Los_Angeles "Silver Lake, Los Angeles"),[[10]](#cite_note-10) which was originally owned by actor and director [Antonio Mareno](/w/index.php?title=Antonio_Mareno&action=edit&redlink=1 "Antonio Mareno (page does not exist)"). At Paramour, Howard produced the [Lucinda Williams](/wiki/Lucinda_Williams "Lucinda Williams") album, *[World Without Tears](/wiki/World_Without_Tears "World Without Tears")*,[[11]](#cite_note-11) which was nominated for a Grammy award.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13) Whilst at the Paramour Howard produced [Vic Chesnutt](/wiki/Vic_Chesnutt "Vic Chesnutt") *[Silver lake](/wiki/Silver_Lake,_Los_Angeles "Silver Lake, Los Angeles")*,[[14]](#cite_note-14) a track with [Eddie Vedder](/wiki/Eddie_Vedder "Eddie Vedder") for soundtrack of the movie, *[I Am Sam](/wiki/I_Am_Sam "I Am Sam")*,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] and the beginnings of [Tom Waits](/wiki/Tom_Waits "Tom Waits") *[Real Gone](/wiki/Real_Gone_(album) "Real Gone (album)")*. Howard finished *[Real Gone](/wiki/Real_Gone_(album) "Real Gone (album)")* at an old school house in northern California.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/91/ParamourEstate.jpeg/250px-ParamourEstate.jpeg)](/wiki/File:ParamourEstate.jpeg)

The Paramour Estate in Silver Lake, Los Angeles
